card_description: The Omega Man is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick explores the idea that humans are "programmed" by the artifact to maintain the simulation."

linked_segments.summary: Dick explores the idea that humans are "programmed" by the artifact to maintain the simulation. Salvation is the liberation from this programming, allowing the "transtemporal self" or the "Omega Man"
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_108.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick discusses the Urgrund’s intervention to transmute the projected reality into a stable divine kingdom. He introduces the "divine marriage" between humanity and Christ/Logos as the final synthesis
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_113.txt
